cacheNotice: Offline demo cache for reviewers. It is not loaded automatically and does not replace live App Store RSS collection or model-driven analysis.
analysisGoal: Evaluate subscription conversion, workout usability, version 4.2.0 regressions, and low-rating reviews.
dataSource: Synthetic JSON/CSV import sample, not real App Store data.

findings.title: Workout timer reliability blocks core session completion
findings.summary: Several low-rating reviews report that workout and rest timers freeze or stop, especially around version 4.2.0 and lock-screen usage.

evidenceQuotes.quote: the workout timer freezes halfway through a session
the workout timer stops and the rest timer never alerts me

findings.uncertainty: Low sample size. One review says reinstalling fixed the crash, so root cause needs device and lifecycle validation.

findings.title: Subscription and trial messaging reduce conversion clarity
findings.summary: Users do not understand what is free, when the trial renews, or why the paywall appears before value is clear.

evidenceQuotes.quote: I cannot tell which workouts are free anymore
The trial page does not explain renewal timing clearly

findings.uncertainty: Low sample size. The sample indicates confusion, not exact conversion loss.

findings.title: Workout usability needs better in-session readability and device sync
findings.summary: Users mention small in-session text and unreliable Apple Watch sync, affecting workout usability and progress trust.

evidenceQuotes.quote: The timer and next exercise text are too small
Apple Watch sync misses completed workouts

findings.uncertainty: Low sample size and two related but distinct usability problems.

requirements.problem: Users can lose workout logs because timers freeze or stop during active sessions.
requirements.goal: Ensure workout and rest timers continue reliably across lock-screen and foreground/background transitions.
nonGoals: Do not redesign the full workout player in V1.0.
acceptanceCriteria: Timer continues for a 30-minute workout after lock/unlock cycles on supported iOS versions.
Rest alert fires within 2 seconds of the expected time after screen lock.
Interrupted sessions preserve elapsed time and workout log state after app resume.

requirements.problem: Users cannot understand free versus paid access or trial renewal timing before subscribing.
requirements.goal: Make subscription value, free access, and trial renewal timing clear before payment intent.
nonGoals: Do not change subscription pricing in V2.0.
acceptanceCriteria: Paywall states free access scope, trial length, renewal date, and cancellation path before purchase CTA.
At least one app value screen is visible before the first subscription prompt in the beginner flow.
QA verifies copy appears correctly for monthly and annual plans.

requirements.problem: In-session readability and progress sync issues reduce workout confidence.
requirements.goal: Improve long-distance readability and make device sync failures visible and recoverable.
nonGoals: Do not rebuild the entire Apple Watch app in V3.0.
acceptanceCriteria: Workout player offers a large-text mode where timer and next exercise labels are readable at 2 meters.
Sync status shows success or retry state within 10 seconds after workout completion.
Manual retry is available when watch sync fails.

testCases.scenario: Verify timer reliability through lock-screen transitions.
testCases.expectedResult: Workout and rest timers continue accurately and session logs are preserved.

testCases.scenario: Verify subscription page explains free access, trial length, renewal timing, and cancellation path.
testCases.expectedResult: User can identify what is free and when billing starts before tapping purchase CTA.

testCases.scenario: Verify large-text workout mode and watch sync recovery.
testCases.expectedResult: Timer is readable from workout distance and failed sync exposes retry state.

traceability.summary: All IDs in this synthetic cache resolve inside the cache. Warning is intentional because the cache is not real App Store evidence.
warnings: Offline demo cache uses synthetic reviews and should not be used for product decisions.
